commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Emojis are used for weather conditions but lack semantic accessibility attributes.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a `<span role="img" aria-label="{conditionText}">` to ensure screen readers announce them correctly.

issues.description: The component is entirely static and lacks subtle polish.
issues.fix: Consider adding subtle hover states (e.g., a slight background color change or scale effect) to the forecast days to make the UI feel more premium.

evaluations.critique: Excellent implementation that perfectly fulfills the prompt requirements with a clean, well-structured layout and prominent typography. To reach production quality, add proper ARIA labels to the weather emojis for screen reader users.

issues.description: Emojis are used for weather conditions but lack semantic accessibility attributes.
issues.fix: Wrap emojis in a `<span role="img" aria-label="{conditionText}">` to ensure screen readers announce them correctly.

issues.description: The component is entirely static and lacks subtle polish.
issues.fix: Consider adding subtle hover states (e.g., a slight background color change or scale effect) to the forecast days to make the UI feel more premium.

evaluation.critique: Excellent implementation that perfectly fulfills the prompt requirements with a clean, well-structured layout and prominent typography. To reach production quality, add proper ARIA labels to the weather emojis for screen reader users.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The component code is truncated at the end, leaving an open `<` tag and missing the final closing tags.
issues.fix: Ensure the component generation completes fully, closing all JSX tags correctly.

issues.description: The required legend displaying each category with its corresponding color is entirely missing.
issues.fix: Add a legend section below the details panel that iterates through categories and renders a color swatch and label for each.

issues.description: Interactive elements on the periodic table are plain `div` tags lacking semantic roles and keyboard support.
issues.fix: Add `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and an `onKeyDown` event handler (checking for Enter/Space) to the element cells.

issues.description: Element cells lack a visual hover state, making them feel less interactive.
issues.fix: Add a hover effect (e.g., slight background darkening or border highlight), which may require extracting the cell to a separate component or using a CSS-in-JS solution rather than pure inline styles.

evaluations.critique: The initial layout utilizing CSS grid for the periodic table is structurally sound and the search opacity filtering is a clever interaction. Unfortunately, the generation was cut off, leaving out the required legend and failing to implement basic accessibility for the interactive grid cells.

issues.description: The component code is truncated at the end, leaving an open `<` tag and missing the final closing tags.
issues.fix: Ensure the component generation completes fully, closing all JSX tags correctly.

issues.description: The required legend displaying each category with its corresponding color is entirely missing.
issues.fix: Add a legend section below the details panel that iterates through categories and renders a color swatch and label for each.

issues.description: Interactive elements on the periodic table are plain `div` tags lacking semantic roles and keyboard support.
issues.fix: Add `role="button"`, `tabIndex={0}`, and an `onKeyDown` event handler (checking for Enter/Space) to the element cells.

issues.description: Element cells lack a visual hover state, making them feel less interactive.
issues.fix: Add a hover effect (e.g., slight background darkening or border highlight), which may require extracting the cell to a separate component or using a CSS-in-JS solution rather than pure inline styles.

Unfortunately, the generation was cut off, leaving out the required legend and failing to implement basic accessibility for the interactive grid cells.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.fix: Add a label like 'Current' or 'Weather' and consider adding a subtle background or container grouping for the forecast strip.

issues.fix: Add a subtle hover effect (e.g., slight lift or background color change) to each forecast item in the strip to signify they are data points.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: The periodic table grid lacks a proper aspect ratio, which may cause it to appear squashed or inconsistently sized depending on screen width.
issues.fix: Apply a min-width to the cell container or set a fixed aspect-ratio on the Card component to ensure cells remain square.

issues.description: Lack of hover states on grid cells makes the interactive nature of the table less intuitive to the user.
issues.fix: Add a CSS hover transition to the Card component to slightly lift or change border color on mouse-over.

issues.description: Grid items are clickable divs without proper button semantics, making them invisible to keyboard users.
issues.fix: Change the clickable wrapper to a <button> element with appropriate aria-labels and reset its default styles.

issues.description: The search functionality visually hides elements, but there is no explicit empty state when no elements match.
issues.fix: Add a conditional render to show a 'No elements found' message if the filtered list length is zero.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional and well-structured, successfully implementing the requested grid layout and filtering logic. It would benefit from improved semantic HTML (using buttons for interactivity) and refined visual feedback for user interactions to reach a professional standard.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: Progress bar calculation is slightly off; using (step - 1) * 50 results in 0%, 50%, 100%. A 3-step process should ideally use steps of 33.3% or 50% depending on the intent, but 0/50/100 is acceptable.
issues.fix: Use (step - 1) * 50 to match the 3-step logic, or adjust to (step - 1) * 33.3 to represent progress accurately across 3 steps.

issues.description: Lack of visual feedback or error messages when email validation fails.
issues.fix: Add a conditional error message below the email input if isValid is false.

issues.description: No fieldset or labels for the avatar selection radio group, making it unclear for screen readers.
issues.fix: Wrap the avatar selection in a group role or fieldset and ensure buttons have aria-labels.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, functional, and maps well to the requirements. It handles state effectively and uses the design system appropriately. Adding clearer validation feedback and improving the semantic grouping for the avatar selection would make it production-ready.
